title: Michael B. Jordan's Oscars Advice and Commentary
text: Michael B. Jordan shared wisdom from his father, saying: Don't expect anything to be handed to you. You do the work, and everything else will figure itself out. The actor encouraged aspiring creatives to maintain authenticity and ambition while being considerate of others. He emphasized: Dream big, man. Dream big, and be kind. Be honest. On competition and personal path, Jordan acknowledged the prestige of the Oscar while advising against unhealthy comparison. He reflected that artists should recognize what's for you is for you, and you can't listen to anybody else, noting he is just walking my path. During his acceptance speech, Jordan paid tribute to Black performers who won Oscars previously, stating he stands because of the people that came before me, citing Sidney Poitier, Denzel Washington, Halle Berry, Jamie Foxx, Forest Whitaker, and Will Smith. Jordan won Best Actor for his dual role in Ryan Coogler's Sinners, which received 16 nominations and won four awards total at the 2026 Oscars ceremony.
